arguments.task: Open official Clay pages and read only first-party content needed to determine: one-line product description, whether Clay has a public API and its authentication methods, pricing/access gating for API use, any rate limit notes, and whether Clay documents an MCP server or Model Context Protocol integration. Prefer canonical official docs, developer, auth, help, pricing, and blog pages. Avoid third-party sources. Return the exact URLs visited and concise extracted evidence for each field.

input.thought: Start an official-source browser run to inspect Clay's site, docs, pricing, and any developer pages.

raw_result.description: Go-to-market data platform for finding data, running agentic workflows, and launching outbound plays.
raw_result.auth_notes: Clay University search results exposed a first-party page titled "Find your Clay API key," supporting token-based API access; no first-party evidence was gathered for OAuth2, API keys as a separate scheme, or Basic auth.
raw_result.gating_reason: Clay has a public pricing page with self-serve plans, including a free starter tier, indicating self-serve access rather than sales-only gating.

api_surface.breadth: Evidence supports API-related functionality and token-based access, but the gathered docs did not establish a broad endpoint catalog; the visible HTTP API page was about calling external APIs from Clay, so Clay's own public API breadth remains unclear.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Clay has an official Clay MCP page describing MCP integration with tools such as ChatGPT, Codex, and Claude.

raw_result.blocker: Official evidence gathered indicates token-based API access and self-serve pricing, but the documentation captured did not clearly expose Clay's own endpoint reference or API breadth, so building a toolkit today would require manual validation of the available endpoints.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No first-party rate-limit documentation was verified in the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official site links directly to Clay MCP, and Clay University search results included first-party entries such as "Does Clay have an API?" and "Find your Clay API key," which strongly signal developer-facing access even though the specific API reference was not captured.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Structured fields requiring human confirmation from stronger first-party evidence: auth_methods and buildability_verdict rely on indirect evidence from Clay University search results rather than a fully read canonical API auth/reference page; api_surface.type is inferred from API-key evidence but Clay's own endpoint documentation was not captured.
